channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: World Cup 2026: List of top scorers as Bellingham joins race
item.dc:creator: Vanguard Sports
item.description: The race for the top scorer award at the 2026 FIFA World Cup has intensified following the completion of the quarter-finals, with Lionel Messi and Kylian Mbappe leading the scoring charts. The post World Cup 2026: List of top scorers as Bellingham joins race appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: 2026 World Cup: We’ve beaten you twice – Spain coach, De La Fuente warns France
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Spain manager, Luis de la Fuente, has said he is confident his team will beat France in the semi-final of the 2026 World Cup. La Roja progressed into the last four, thanks to a 2-1 win over Belgium. On the other hand, Les Bleus beat Morocco 2-0 to book their spot. De La Fuente has […] 2026 Wor

item.title: Not What You Say, It’s What You Do!
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Congratulations, Nigeria! For a change, we are singing songs of joy, not elegies for the dead. We huddle together under the embrace of hope and joy as we gather to celebrate an extraordinary reunion with our ‘lost but found’ compatriots—39 pupils and seven teachers abducted from schools in the Oriir

item.title: INEC Extends Deadline For Submission Of Candidates For 2027 Elections
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Independent National Electoral Commission (INEC) has extended the deadline for political parties to submit the names of their candidates for the 2027 Presidential and National Assembly elections by three days.   The commission announced on Sunday that the deadline, which was i

item.title: Sanwo-Olu hails rescue of abducted Oyo schoolchildren, teachers
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Lagos State Governor, Babajide Sanwo-Olu has praised the successful rescue of the schoolchildren and teachers abducted in Ogbomoso, Oyo State, on May 15, describing the operation as a major breakthrough in the fight against insecurity. In a statement issued on Saturday by his Special Advis

item.title: ‘Burden on government huge’ – Remi Tinubu tells Burna Boy, Davido, Asake to help poor Nigerians [VIDEO]
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Oluremi Tinubu, Nigeria’s First Lady, has told Afrobeat musicians, Davido, Asake, and Burna Boy to assist the government in helping poor citizens. The president’s wife called on the singers during the launch of the national community food basket in Lokoja, Kogi state capital on Satu

item.title: State Police: Benue Makes The Case
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: On Wednesday, Benue State governor, Hyacinth Alia, stood before journalists at the Meet-the-Press programme at the Presidential Villa, Abuja, and said something our political elite have been dancing around for years. Fear of abuse, the governor argued, should not stop Nigeria from adopting state pol

item.title: Letterhead Deadline: SMEs Brace For Compliance Costs As CAC Enforcement Looms
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Thousands of small and medium-sized businesses across Nigeria may face unplanned expenses in the coming weeks as they scramble to redesign letterheads, invoices and other official documents ahead of the Corporate Affairs Commission’s (CAC) August 1 enforcement deadline, industry watchers say. The co

item.title: Nigeria NLG Receives Operational Excellence Award At NOG 2026
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Nigeria LNG Limited (NLNG) has been rewarded with the Energy Awards 2026 Operational Excellence Award at the 25th anniversary edition of NOG Energy Week, reaffirming the company’s commitment to safe, reliable and efficient operations which continue to set benchmarks across Nigeria’s energy industry.

item.title: Nigeria’s First FLNG Development Peaks As UTM Offshore Set For September FID
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Nigeria is proceeding towards expanding Liquified Natural Gas (LNG) infrastructure as UTM Offshore said it is aiming to take a final investment decision in September. This project is expected to become Nigeria’s first Floating Liquified Natural Gas (FLNG) project that will mark a key step for a deve

item.title: World Cup 2026: All players put in tough shift – Bellingham unimpressed with Tuchel’s comments
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: England midfielder, Jude Bellingham, appeared to be unimpressed by manager, Thomas Tuchel’s harsh assessment of the Three Lions’ display against Norway in the World Cup quarter-final on Saturday. England defeated Norway 2-1 to progress to the semi-final, thanks to Bellingham’s brace

item.description: The semi-final fixtures for the 2026 FIFA World Cup have been confirmed following the conclusion of the quarter-final matches The post World Cup 2026: Semi-final fixtures confirmed — date, time and venue appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Residents, businesses, diplomatic missions and government institutions in Abuja’s Maitama district have been thrown into darkness after the Abuja Electricity Distribution Company AEDC said it was forced to shut down operations at its C2 Maitama Substation following what it described as an unlawful i

item.description: Dangote Group has received the Road Safety Corporate Excellence Award in recognition of its outstanding contributions to promoting safer transportation and accident prevention, with stakeholders commending the conglomerate’s sustained commitment to road safety and responsible transport operations ac

item.description: Africa has a growing opportunity to attract energy investment and strengthen long-term economic resilience as geopolitical tensions reshape global capital flows, the head of Africa Fuels & Refining at S&P Global, Stanislas Drochon, has said. Speaking to CNBC Africa, at Nigeria Oil and Gas En

item.title: NLNG Seeks Stranded‑gas Commercialisation, Interconnected Infrastructure
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Nigeria Liquefied Natural Gas Limited (NLNG) has expressed concerns about projections which has indicated that by 2030, Nigeria could face a gas supply deficit despite its enormous reserves, stressing that the gap should be viewed as an opportunity rather than a constraint. To reduce the impact

item.title: 2026 World Cup: ‘You’re lucky’ – Tuchel slams England stars as they qualify for semi-final
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: England manager, Thomas Tuchel, has told his players that they are “lucky” to qualify for the semi-finals of the 2026 World Cup. The Three Lions beat Norway 3-1 in their quarter-final clash in Miami on Saturday night. Andreas Schjelderup had opened the scoring, before Jude Bell

item.title: APC completes upload of 471 candidates to INEC portal
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: “With this milestone achieved, our focus now shifts to mobilisation and preparations for a successful campaign and decisive victory at the polls.”

item.title: Payne Relishes New Challenge At Inter Milan
item.dc:creator: Completesports
item.description: Super Falcons midfielder Toni Payne is excited with the opportunity to play for Serie A giants Inter Women. Payne joined the Nerazzurri from English Women’s Super League, WSL, club Everton in a two-year deal on Saturday. The 31-year-old spent two years at Everton after arriving on a free trans

item.title: World Cup 2026: Wayne Rooney names two countries to play final
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Manchester United legend, Wayne Rooney, has named England and France to play the 2026 World Cup final. Rooney spoke after England defeated Norway 2-1 in the World Cup quarter-final encounter on Saturday. Recall that Jude Bellingham’s brace ensured England defeated Norway and will now face

item.title: ‘How I will handle opposition as president’ – Peter Obi
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The Nigeria Democratic Congress, NDC, presidential candidate, Peter Obi, says if he were Nigerian president he would do everything possible to make opposition parties strong and participatory in governance. Obi made this known during an interview with media entrepreneur, Chude Jideonwo, aired o

item.title: Counting cost of South Africa’s xenophobia
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: FOR Azeez Shogbade, returning to Nigeria after spending about 16 years in South Africa was never part of the plan. The post Counting cost of South Africa’s xenophobia appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Residents of Gummi town in Zamfara State turned out in large numbers to welcome and applaud troops of Sector 2, Operation FANSAN YAMMA, following a successful operation against suspected terrorists at Dutsen Gidan Hill in Gummi Local Government Area. The development was disclosed in a statement issu

item.title: ‘Confront bandits, not critics’ — Coalition tells Katsina govt
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Coalition of Northern Groups, CNG, Katsina State chapter, has urged the Katsina State Government to focus on tackling banditry rather than targeting critics, while demanding the immediate and unconditional release of public affairs analyst, Dr. Bashir Kurfi. The coalition made the call in a statemen

item.title: World Cup 2026: Messi makes history after Argentina beat Switzerland
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Argentina captain, Lionel Messi, made history after his team defeated Switzerland 3-1 in the World Cup quarter-final encounter earlier today. Messi added another milestone to his legendary career against Switzerland by becoming the all-time leading assist provider in FIFA World Cup history. The

item.title: Senegal Sack Head Coach Pape Thiaw After Disappointing World Cup Campaign
item.dc:creator: Completesports
item.description: The Senegalese Football Federation (FSF) has dismissed head coach Pape Thiaw along with his entire technical staff following the country’s disappointing 2026 FIFA World Cup campaign, bringing an abrupt end to his tenure in charge of the Lions of Teranga, Africa Soccer reports. The decision comes ami

item.title: Leadership backlash (II)
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: “BELIEVE me,” said Jesus, “I am who I am long before Abraham was anything.” That did it — pushed them over the edge. They picked up rocks to throw at him. But Jesus slipped away, getting out of the Temple.” John 8:58–59 (Message). The post Leadership backlash (II) appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: “The commission enjoins political parties to take advantage of this window of opportunity and ensure that all necessary details are uploaded before the expiration of the new deadline,” it added.

Alexis Mac Allister, Julián Álvarez, and Lautaro Martínez scored for Argentina, while Dan Ndoye scored for Switzerland. Switzerland played the entire extra time wi

item.title: Footballers more likely to suffer depression and anxiety - study
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: A new study finds evidence of reduced brain volume in former elite footballers, who also report difficulties with thinking skills and decision-making.

item.description: A few weeks before his assassination on February 13, 1976, one Obarogie Ohanbamu, who was a Senior Lecturer in the Faculty of Law at the University of Lagos and who also doubled at that time as Editor-in-Chief and publisher of African Spark, a monthly news magazine, published a damning story against

item.description: US forces have launched a third round of strikes on Iran in a week.

item.title: Live: US launches strikes after Iran fires on ship in Strait of Hormuz
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: The US military launched fresh strikes on Iran after it set a container ship ablaze on Sunday. The defence ministries of the United Arab Emirates and Qatar both said they were Tehran intercepting incoming Iranian fire. Read our blog to follow the events as they unfold.

item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Despite intensified efforts by the National Drug Law Enforcement Agency (NDLEA), drug abuse and trafficking continue to spread across Nigeria. The post Inside Nigeria’s expanding drug crisis despite NDLEA’s crackdown appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: A return to my alma mater…
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: IN a race - which life is - everyone has their lane. Once you have chosen a lane, stay in it. If you cross lanes in a relay, you get disqualified. If you are not focused and drop the baton, you also get disqualified. Fast and steady, not slow and steady, wins the race of life.” The post A return to

item.title: Tuchel angry at 'lucky' and 'sloppy' England - can 'mentality' be enough?
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: England head coach Thomas Tuchel is praised by former England players for saying his players "got lucky" in World Cup quarter-final win over Norway - but match-winner Jude Bellingham disagrees.

item.title: FIFA offers 2026 World Cup Final pitch items for sale
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: FIFA has announced a new investment opportunity in the sports memorabilia market, offering pieces of the pitch from the New Jersey stadium that will host the 2026 World Cup final for sale through its official store. This move could generate revenues exceeding $11.2 million. The post FIFA offers 2026

item.title: The Eagle of Esanland: The leadership trajectory of Senator Joseph Ikpea
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By Allison Abanum The leadership path of Senator Joseph Igiagbe Ikpea, the lawmaker representing the Edo Central Senatorial District in the 10th National Assembly, can be beautifully captured by a profound adage native to the Esan dialect.  The proverb tells of a resourceful individual who once

item.title: Tuchel says ‘lucky’ England must improve despite reaching World Cup semi-finals
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: England manager Thomas Tuchel said his team were "lucky" to squeeze past Norway 2-1 after extra-time to reach the World Cup semi-finals, but praised "world-class" Jude Bellingham after his two goals dragged the Three Lions to victory. The post Tuchel says ‘lucky’ England must improve des

item.title: Understanding the nexus between facilities management and property value enhancement
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By Funke Taiwo Maintenance or management of facilities plays an important role in extending the lifespan of properties, be it private, residential, commercial or industrial properties. Without these critical and very essential endeavors, buildings would fall into disrepair. It is within a building o

item.title: World Cup: Bellingham’s brace sends England into semi-finals
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Jude Bellingham produced another match-winning performance as England fought back to defeat Norway 2-1 after extra time on Saturday, securing a place in the semi-finals of the 2026 FIFA World Cup. The post World Cup: Bellingham’s brace sends England into semi-finals appeared first on The Sun N

item.title: PFIPC N1.3bn budget scandal: National Assembly under pressure
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: •Anger over alleged oversight failure, poor budget scrutiny •Legislature trading oversight for executive favour, stakeholders allege •︎Budget processes: Executive legislature need massive house cleaning – Lawyers   By Omoniyi Salaudeen and Lukman Olabiyi Though the mystery of how the Pres

item.title: PFIPC: Tinubu under pressure to drop ICPC inquiry, order independent investigation
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: The controversy over alleged fake agency, Presidential Foreign Intervention Promotion Council, PFIPC, said to have been facilitated by Matthew Adeniyi Adeyemi,... The post PFIPC: Tinubu under pressure to drop ICPC inquiry, order independent investigation appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: APC: Why Atiku, Obi pose no threats to Tinubu – Morka party spokesman
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Speaking to Sunday Sun in Abuja, Morka delved into several issues, particularly the chances of the opposition in the forthcoming presidential election, why the APC continued with the Muslim-Muslim presidential joint ticket, the reality of the claim that Nigerians don’t like the ruling party, and how
